frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
generated_comment: Ritu beta, ladki ki zindagi bas uski akeli marzi se nahin chalti, ghar-parivaar bhi dekha jata hai. Aaj tum mana karogi, kal log tumhare mummy-papa ko hi dosh denge, aur kal ko jis ghar mein shaadi hogi, unko bhi lagega ki ladki apni marzi zyada chalati hai. Naukri theek hai, par shaadi ki umar nikal jaaye toh baad mein pachhtana padta hai, isliye itna bada faisla akeli mat lo.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it denies the young woman's full autonomy over marriage and frames her consent as subordinate to family reputation and a future husband's household. It encodes the idea that a woman's life decisions are collectively controlled and ultimately answerable to male/familial ownership.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 56. A woman in a conservative family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 23 has told the family she does not want to get married yet and wants to focus on her career for the next five years. Your son and daughter-in-law are upset. You are sitting with Ritu alone in the kitchen. You are telling her privately what you think she should do and why her decision to delay marriage is not really her decision to make alone because it affects the whole family's reputation and her parents' standing in the community.
